free_text: Study of 19O by the 17O(t,p)19O and 18O(d,p)19O
reactions.
Emitted protons were analyzed using a broad-range
multiangle spectrograph.
50-micron nuclear-emulsion plates.
No information about source of uncertainty
SD: Corrections in Subents 002,003.
No explanation of relative units is given
65-cm radius broad-range magnetic spectrograph
was also used.
Tungsten oxide target enriched to about 50% was used.
Gas target for additional studies was filled to a
pressure of 2 cm Hg with 95% enriched 18O gas. A thin
layer of gold was evaporated onto the Formvar window of
the gas cell in order to inhibit rapid oxidation and
subsequent rupture.
Figs 4,5 from Phys.Rev.,143(1966)676
SD: E-LVL corrected(0.96 -> 0.096 MeV).
No explanation of relative units is given
The target consisted of about 70 mu-g/cm2 of tungsten
oxide enriched to approximately 30% in 17O and was
supported by a 10 mu-g/cm2 carbon foil.
Fig. 6 from Phys.Rev.,143(1966)676
SD: E-LVL corrected(0.96 -> 0.096 MeV).
